what_it_does: This file is a build specification for the POPCORN/KERNELS subsystem: it defines a new channel item-type called a KERNEL (an outside perspective) and lays out the staged work to wire generative and adversarial run-paths into the existing operator-cycle/decision-surface.
observations: Defines a KERNEL as an outside perspective curated to an instance, with no stake in consensus and no preconception to satisfy.
Specifies two valences: GENERATE un-preconceived ideas/designs, or ADVERSARIALLY attack the fabric's done/connected claims.
Describes the kernel as composable via perspective · input-spread · prompt · substrate · threshold registry fields.

Lists existing engine capabilities considered ~80% complete: run_panel/run_jury, run_draft/run_items/run_reduce/run_cascade, local-4B fleet concurrency, fresh-session spawn, item-type/decision pattern, and dragnet chunk reading.
Provides a ~20% build list sequenced as C (certain-landscape dragnet over company files), kernel item-type mirroring decisions, run-paths for both valences, and an orchestration loop.
States an explicit build sequence D → C → A → B, where D is this spec, C is landscape grounding, A is generative kernel, and B is adversarial kernel.
Notes company-directory drift: /home/tim/company is the only live runtime; other copies are stale/explorations to merge or retire.
Includes specific code-location citations (cognition.py, mcp_face/server.py, dragnet_extract.py, session_supervisor) for existing and planned wiring.
Emphasizes folding kernels onto the operator-cycle by reusing decision cards, feedback marks, feed-builder, and elevation-to-Tim mechanics.
standouts: The kernel is designed as a first-class channel item-type alongside decisions, reusing the same card/feed/feedback infrastructure rather than building a separate surface.
The dual valence design (generative vs adversarial) frames the kernel as both an idea generator and a standing confirmation-bias fix.
The recursive closing claim: the adversarial kernel should harden the fabric that is building popcorn, including popcorn itself.
The staged D→C→A→B sequence makes landscape grounding a prerequisite for generative work and adversarial work a standing layer.
conventions: Follows a build-spec structure: one-line primitive, what exists, what to wire, sequence, and fold-back summary.
Uses internal citation style mixing file:line references, wiki-style memory links, and uppercase labels.
Uses arrow notation (D→C→A→B) to express knowledge-dependency ordering.
Mixes implementation detail, design rationale, and organizational cleanup in a single document.
References a named authority/taste source ('Tim's direction', 'Tim's words', 'elevate-to-Tim') as part of the workflow.
concerns: Heavy reliance on specific code line numbers that will go stale as the codebase changes.
Asserts the system is ~80% built without defining concrete acceptance criteria for the remaining ~20%.
Conflates technical build work with organizational cleanup (merge/retire stale company-directory copies).
The distinction between 'Tim's words' and a 'formalised prompt' is not operationally defined.
The recursive self-auditing claim risks circular validation if boundaries and halting conditions are not specified.
notes: A build-ready spec that wants to add an outside-perspective 'kernel' item-type to the existing decision channel, with a clear staged sequence and a recursive adversarial safety loop.
questions: What is the exact schema for a kernels/<id>.py registry entry?
What is the formalized prompt that will drive the certain-landscape dragnet over company files?
How is the relevance threshold in the dragnet determined and tuned?
What does 'elevate-to-Tim' mean mechanically in the operator-cycle?
Which specific files or directories are the stale company-directory copies to be merged or retired?
What is the relationship between this spec and CHANNEL-LOOP-BOARD.md / [[project-popcorn-kernels]]?
What are the exact acceptance criteria that mark the transition from ~80% to complete?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is the build spec for POPCORN/KERNELS and delivers a primitive, existing-state inventory, wiring list, sequence, and operator-cycle integration plan.
apparent_themes: Reuse of existing channel/card/feed infrastructure for new semantic item types
Local-4B breadth combined with fresh-session depth
intention_signals: The file is trying to formalize a mechanism that injects an external, non-consensus perspective into the operator cycle so the system can both generate ideas the operator did not specify and adversarially refute the fabric's own claims, with the strongest results surfaced to a human decision-maker.
novelty: It treats an outside perspective as a registry-defined, first-class channel item-type that reuses decision machinery, and it explicitly proposes that the adversarial kernel audit the very fabric building the kernel system.
contribution: A concrete, staged build plan for adding a kernel item-type and two run-paths (generative and adversarial) to an existing operator-cycle, reusing card/feed/feedback infrastructure and local-4B plus fresh-session compute patterns.
summary_for_embedding: POPCORN-BUILD.md is a build specification that defines the KERNEL item-type as an outside perspective for generating ideas or adversarially refuting claims, and sequences the wiring of kernel run-paths into an existing operator-cycle.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to guide implementation of the POPCORN/KERNELS subsystem by reusing existing decision-channel infrastructure and staging work across landscape grounding, generative kernels, and adversarial kernels.
